health_zone_table.basis: SitRep44 Table 2 (27 June 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF and reconciled to Table 1 / province totals 1274/360. A new affected health zone (Mandima, Ituri) is reported, raising the affected-zone count to 35/104. The table carries 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified; these remain an unventilated residual and are not distributed to named health zones. PDF sha256 122a15ac6296926dd1ff8c05e0c605fd8f4612943145c3097264d3df66d897f1.

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1257 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation = 1274; all 360 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep44 section 4.3: Ituri 162 collected/analyzed / 45 positive; Nord-Kivu 115 received / 99 analyzed / 2 positive / 16 under analysis (11 Beni, 5 Butembo); Sud-Kivu 4 collected/analyzed / 0 positive. Positivity uses 47 positives over 265 analyzed samples. The Nord-Kivu challenge table still flags a sample backlog and delayed diagnosis.

care_continuity: End-of-day isolation stood at 502 patients (208 confirmed, 294 suspected) at 96.7% global occupancy.
Ituri isolation occupancy was 90.9% with 338 patients isolated; Nord-Kivu was 138.8% with 143 (suspected-case saturation flagged in Butembo, Kalunguta, Masereka, Musienene); Sud-Kivu 47.7% with 21.
One escape was recorded in Ituri during the cycle; 15 recovered exits were recorded, all in Ituri.
highlights: 47 new confirmed cases including 12 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-27, in Ituri (45 including 11 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (2 including 1 death).

New confirmed cases were distributed across Rwampara (19), Bunia (6), Mongbwalu (5), Nizi (3), Komanda (3), Drodro (2), Bambu (2), Damas (1), Lita (1), Mangala (1), Mandima (1), Nyankunde (1) in Ituri, and Katwa (2) in Nord-Kivu.
10 additional deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE patients in Ituri (Mongbwalu 5, CTE Elikya/Bunia 2, Nyankunde 1, Komanda 1, CTE ISTM/Rwampara 1), bringing confirmed deaths to 360.
A new affected health zone, Mandima (Ituri), was reported on 2026-06-27, raising affected zones to 35/104; Ituri now reports 23/36 zones touched.
15 patients were declared recovered in Ituri after negative control tests, bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 178 (168 Ituri, 8 Nord-Kivu, 2 Sud-Kivu).
laboratory: SitRep44 reports Ituri 162 samples collected and analyzed with 45 positive (27.7%).
Nord-Kivu received 115 samples and analyzed 99, with 2 positive; 16 remain under analysis (11 Beni, 5 Butembo). Sud-Kivu collected and analyzed 4, all negative.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 44 reports through 2026-06-27 and was published on the INSP WordPress site on 2026-06-28T21:41:42 (post 25064; PDF media 25065).
No SitRep 43 is visible in the latest INSP posts/media API; the series preserves SitRep 43 as absent/skipped and SitRep44 supersedes SitRep42 directly.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 35 of 104 health zones; Mandima (Ituri) is the newly affected zone this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1165 of 1274 DRC confirmed cases (91.4%).

Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 54.7%, led by Oicha (66.7%), Kyondo (66.7%), Katwa (62.2%), and Beni (59.1%); 100% lethality observed in low-count Vuhovi and Musienene.
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 1,283 alerts were reported and 1,271 investigated, producing a 99.1% investigation rate; Ituri 97.6% (290/297), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (962/962), Sud-Kivu 79.2% (19/24).
239 suspected cases of the day were identified, including 70 deceased validated alerts.
9,145 contacts were under follow-up and 7,964 were seen, producing 87.1% follow-up coverage.
260 new contacts were listed (210 across 6 Ituri health zones and 50 across 2 Nord-Kivu health zones); 50 contacts completed 21-day follow-up successfully in Nord-Kivu.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 12; new alerts 1271; 260 new contacts listed (210 in 6 Ituri HZs, 50 in 2 Nord-Kivu HZs); 50 contacts completed 21-day follow-up in Nord-Kivu.

challenges.finding: Nord-Kivu CTE isolation occupancy reached 138.8% (saturation in Butembo, Kalunguta, Masereka, Musienene) and Ituri stood at 90.9%, against an estimated gap of about 20 isolation centers.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 87.1% versus the 95% target (a 7.9 pp gap).
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet precisely ventilated by health zone.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, duplicate cleanup, field verification, and reclassification.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: The SitRep44 challenge table reports 78 health-worker infections and 18 deaths (CFR 23.1%).
challenges.response: Reinforce IPC/WASH supplies (PPE, chlorine), supervision, and training.

challenges.finding: Nord-Kivu carries a laboratory sample backlog (16 under analysis) and late diagnosis amid a 54.7% provincial CFR.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations in high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: 260 new contacts listed (210 in 6 Ituri HZs, 50 in 2 Nord-Kivu HZs); 50 contacts completed 21-day follow-up in Nord-Kivu. Ituri/Nord-Kivu counts flagged in-validation (harmonization not finalized); Nord-Kivu rate computed only for reporting ZS (80% completeness).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones
Relieve Nord-Kivu isolation saturation (138.8% occupancy) and Ituri load (90.9%); close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers
Improve contact follow-up (87.1% vs 95% target) and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog given the 54.7% provincial CFR

preserved_conflicts: SitRep44 headline is DRC-only (1274 confirmed / 360 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.

The 1,274 confirmed total is marked re-actualized after DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization; treat the +71 confirmed and +39 confirmed deaths vs SitRep42 as official revised cumulative deltas spanning two report-days (SitRep43 was not published), not single-day incident-line-list deltas.
No SitRep 43 is visible in the latest INSP posts/media API; the series preserves SitRep 43 as absent/skipped (joining the existing 003 and 029 source-number gaps) unless later backfilled.
SitRep44 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
Mandima (Ituri) is newly affected on 2026-06-27 with 1 confirmed case / 0 deaths; affected zones advance from 34 to 35 of 104.

Ituri recorded 10 additional deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients (Mongbwalu 5, CTE Elikya/Bunia 2, Nyankunde 1, Komanda 1, CTE ISTM/Rwampara 1); with the 12 deaths among the 47 new confirmed cases (Ituri 11, Nord-Kivu 1), the confirmed-death total advances to 360.
Daily suspected cases (239) and the 70 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases (2 deaths, 11 recoveries) per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard (last reviewed 2026-06-21); carried forward this cycle as INSP reports DRC only, so country-scope is 1294 confirmed / 362 deaths / 189 recovered.

A Nia-Nia footnote records that on 25 June, 2 listed contacts of a Nia-Nia confirmed case were found symptomatic in Wamba ZS (Haut-Uele) and tested positive; this is an Ituri-linked cross-zone signal, not a Haut-Uele denominator addition.
Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26, suggesting no recent transmission; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death across Miti-Murhesa.

review_notes: SitRep #044 (report date 2026-06-27; INSP WordPress post 25064 published 2026-06-28T21:41:42; embedded PDF media 25065). PDF sha256 122a15ac6296926dd1ff8c05e0c605fd8f4612943145c3097264d3df66d897f1, 11 pages. Title N044/MVB_27/06/2026; treated as report-date 2026-06-27.

No SitRep 43 is visible in the latest INSP posts/media API; the series preserves SitRep 43 as absent/skipped (alongside the existing 003 and 029 source-number gaps) and SitRep44 supersedes SitRep42 directly, so the cumulative deltas span two report-days.

DRC headline 1274 confirmed / 360 confirmed deaths (CFR 28.3%); +71 confirmed and +39 confirmed deaths vs SitRep42. On 27 June, 47 new confirmed (Ituri 45, Nord-Kivu 2) carried 12 deaths; a further 10 deaths occurred among previously confirmed Ituri CTE patients.
The 1,274 total is flagged re-actualized after DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization; deltas are official revised deltas.

A new affected health zone, Mandima (Ituri), is reported (1 confirmed / 0 deaths). Affected-zone count advances from 34 to 35 of 104; Ituri now 23/36 touched. New map geometry/centroid is required for Mandima this cycle.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1274 confirmed / 360 deaths: 1257 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with all 360 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Recovered advanced to 178 DRC cumulative (+30 over two report-days; 168 Ituri / 8 Nord-Kivu / 2 Sud-Kivu). Uganda anchor carried forward at 20 cumulative cases / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (last reviewed 2026-06-21; the dashboard was not re-fetched this cycle), so country-scope is 1294 confirmed / 362 deaths / 189 recovered.

Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: Ituri 162 collected/analyzed, 45 positive; Nord-Kivu 115 received / 99 analyzed, 2 positive, 16 under analysis (11 Beni, 5 Butembo); Sud-Kivu 4 collected/analyzed, 0 positive (265 analyzed / 47 positive overall, 17.7%). The Nord-Kivu backlog and delayed diagnosis remain a flagged challenge.

Operational data captured: 1,283 alerts (1,271 investigated, 99.1%), 239 suspected cases of the day (70 deceased validated alerts), 9,145 contacts under follow-up (87.1%), 502 in isolation (208 confirmed / 294 suspected, 96.7% occupancy; Nord-Kivu 138.8% saturation), 260 new contacts (210 across 6 Ituri zones, 50 across 2 Nord-Kivu zones), PoC activation 95.7% (66/69) with 152,371 travelers passed.

Nia-Nia footnote retained: on 25 June, 2 listed contacts of a Nia-Nia case were found symptomatic in Wamba ZS (Haut-Uele) and tested positive; recorded as an Ituri-linked cross-zone signal only. The France imported case from prior cycles remains context only.
